items.front: What are the exact radio terms for Distress and Urgency, and how should each be transmitted?
items.back: Distress: 'Mayday' preferably repeated three times. Urgency: 'Pan-Pan' in the same manner (preferably repeated three times). Per 10-1-1b.

items.front: If a pilot never says 'Mayday' or 'Pan-Pan' but the controller believes an emergency exists, what is the rule?
items.back: Handle it as though it were an emergency. Per 10-1-1c.

items.front: What five items may an Emergency Autoland system transmit?
items.back: 1. Callsign. 2. That Emergency Autoland has been activated. 3. Position (mileage and direction) relative to a nearby airport. 4. Intended emergency landing airport and planned landing runway. 5. ETE to the emergency landing airport. Per 10-1-2b.

items.front: When may emergency-handling responsibility be transferred to another facility?
items.back: Only when you feel better handling of the emergency will result. Not simply because the aircraft crosses a boundary. Per 10-1-4a.

items.front: What four categories trigger Center ALNOTs?
items.back: 1. Overdue/missing IFR aircraft. 2. Aircraft in emergency in the Center's area. 3. Combination VFR/IFR or airfiled IFR aircraft with no contact 30 min after IFR clearance request. 4. Overdue/missing special VFR aircraft. Per note to 10-1-4.

items.front: What are the two mandatory conditions before aircraft may join up in formation during an emergency?
items.back: 1. Pilots are familiar with and capable of formation flight. 2. They can communicate with one another AND have visual contact with each other. Per 10-1-4f.

items.front: How must non-SAR aircraft get closer than radar separation standards allow during an emergency escort?
items.back: The maneuver must be accomplished visually by the aircraft involved. Per 10-1-4f2.
